channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Dropping Fernandez - a sign of Rosenior's authority or a big risk?
item.description: Enzo Fernandez has given Chelsea boss Liam Rosenior the first major internal disciplinary issue of his brief tenure at Stamford Bridge - BBC Sport analyses the situation.

item.title: Alexander-Arnold fails to ease Tuchel concerns as Kane stars
item.description: Thomas Tuchel's doubts about Trent Alexander-Arnold and reliance on Harry Kane are confirmed on the England manager's Madrid mission.

item.title: McInnes happy to play Hearts' title deciders in a car park
item.description: Heart of Midlothian head coach Derek McInnes would be happy to "play them in a car park" as he responds to complaints from some fans about the fixtures for the final five games of the Scottish Premiership season.

item.title: World's best keeper? Raya helps Arsenal get back on track
item.description: David Raya is the best goalkeeper in the world, according to his Arsenal team-mates - and the Spaniard played a key role in his side's 1-0 win at Sporting.

item.title: 'We've become quite experienced in negativity'
item.description: Arne Slot says Liverpool have no chance against Paris St-Germain unless his side turns up for 90 minutes - after disagreeing with Virgil Van Dijk's comments about the team giving up.

item.title: Why La Liga teams will play in retro shirts this weekend
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team explain why many La Liga sides will be playing their next matches wearing retro kits.

item.title: How WSL aims to bridge academy gap - and why it needs to
item.description: Chelsea manager Sonia Bompastor raised concerns over the gap between academy football and the first team - so is it an issue in the game and how could it be solved?

item.title: Scotland & England fans in Boston face train fare hike
item.description: Scotland and England fans face a huge price hike if they travel by train to World Cup group stage matches in Boston.

item.title: Premier League secures fifth Champions League spot
item.description: The Premier League will have at least five teams in the Champions League next season after securing a European Performance Spot for the second straight year.

item.title: Legendary manager Lucescu dies fives days after resigning as Romania coach
item.description: Legendary manager Mircea Lucescu dies aged 80, five days after stepping down as Romania's head coach.

item.title: Spurs want Trafford as Vicario replacement - Wednesday's gossip
item.description: Tottenham choose Guglielmo Vicario replacement, Liverpool monitor Italy defender, Manchester United set to miss out on England midfielder.

item.title: Neil, Lampard, Jakirovic & Hellberg up for manager award
item.description: Frank Lampard, Alex Neil, Sergej Jakirovic and Kim Hellberg are shortlisted for the EFL manager of the season award.

item.title: Hearts and Celtic given potential final-day title decider
item.description: Celtic to host Heart of Midlothian in potential final-day title decider as the Scottish Premiership's post-split fixtures are revealed.

item.title: Thousands of England & Scotland World Cup tickets on resale at inflated prices
item.description: England and Scotland fans hoping to get to ticket to this summer's World Cup finals face paying vastly inflated prices through Fifa's official resale platform.

item.title: Celtic lift suspension of Green Brigade fans group
item.description: Celtic lift the suspension of the Green Brigade supporters group "with effect from Saturday's home game against St Mirren".

item.title: Arsenal 'had a point to prove' after 'difficult period' - Arteta
item.description: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta says his side "had a point to prove" following back-to-back defeats and praises his side's reaction, as Kai Havertz's stoppage-time winner secures a first-leg advantage in their Champions League quarter-final against Sporting.

item.title: Did quadruple ambition set bar too high for Arsenal?
item.description: Monday Night Club's Rory Smith explains why the quadruple was too much for Arsenal.

item.title: Ramsey memories are made of this
item.description: BBC Sport Wales takes a look back at some of Aaron Ramsey's career highlights as he announces his retirement.

item.title: Leeds beat West Ham on penalties in thriller to reach FA Cup semis
item.description: Leeds United survive West Ham's fightback from 2-0 down to 2-2 as a penalty shootout victory sends the West Yorkshire side to their first FA Cup semi-final since 1987.

item.title: Struijk sends Leeds to first FA Cup semi-final since 1987
item.description: Watch Pascal Struijk score the winning penalty in Leeds United's shootout win against West Ham to send the West Yorkshire side to their first FA Cup semi-final in 39 years.

item.title: Beauty forged in pain: Ramsey, Wales' 'best midfielder ever'
item.description: Aaron Ramsey will be remembered as a great after overcoming shattering setbacks to become a brilliant midfielder for Wales and Arsenal.

item.title: Maguire's new deal underlines revival under Carrick
item.description: Former captain Harry Maguire signs a new one-year contract with Manchester United.

item.title: 'Nobody should be priced out' - why Liverpool protests matter to rival fans
item.description: Liverpool supporters' action against ticket price rises should be supported by rivals, according to the FSA

item.title: Amad backs Carrick for Manchester United job
item.description: Manchester United winger Amad Diallo backs interim boss Michael Carrick to become the club's permanent manager.

item.title: Minnesota deny Rodriguez has rare muscle wasting condition
item.description: Minnesota United deny rumours that midfielder James Rodriguez is suffering from a potentially life-threatening muscle-wasting condition.

item.title: Lijnders on replacing 'unique' Silva
item.description: Manchester City assistant manager Pep Lijnders discusses captain Bernardo Silva's future at the club, saying "every good story comes to an end".

item.title: Disasi draws West Ham level with 96th-minute equaliser
item.description: West Ham's Axel Disasi athletically prods an equaliser six minutes into stoppage time against Leeds United, forcing extra time in the FA Cup quarter-final.

item.title: Herrick's dramatic debut and Struijk's winner - full penalty shootout
item.description: Watch all the penalties from West Ham's penalty shootout against Leeds United in their FA Cup quarter-final at London Stadium.

item.title: 'Fancy footwork' - Tanaka puts Leeds ahead against West Ham
item.description: Leeds United's Ao Tanaka starts and finishes a well-worked move to give his side the lead against West Ham in their FA Cup quarter-final.

item.title: Leeds never do it the easy way - Farke
item.description: Leeds United manager Daniel Farke says his side "never do it the easy way" after letting go of a two-goal lead against West Ham before beating the London side on penalties to reach their first FA Cup final since 1987.

item.title: Ex-footballer Barton denies attack at golf club
item.description: The ex-Manchester City, Newcastle United, QPR, Burnley and Rangers player is accused of assault.

item.title: Fifa starts disciplinary action over Spain v Egypt chants
item.description: Fifa opens disciplinary proceedings against the Spanish FA after "Islamophobic and xenophobic" chants during a friendly between Spain and Egypt last month.

item.title: 54 years, 48 leagues, 2,000 grounds - the ultimate football odyssey
item.description: BBC Sport travels with the UK's ultimate groundhopper to the final match of a decades-long, history-making journey.

item.title: Bay Collective become majority owners of Sunderland Women
item.description: Multi-club ownership organisation Bay Collective has reached an agreement to acquire a majority stake in Sunderland Women.

item.title: Barry gets first England call-up as Godfrey injured
item.description: Bay FC forward Keira Barry receives her first call-up to the England squad for the upcoming Women's World Cup qualifiers against Spain and Iceland.

item.title: West Ham 'must react' after 'tough' loss to Leeds - Nuno
item.description: West Ham manager Nuno Espirito Santo says his side's FA Cup quarter-final defeat by Leeds on penalties is "sad" but his side "must react" as they face Wolverhampton Wanderers in a crucial relegation clash in their next league game.

item.title: Should Leeds have been awarded a penalty for this late challenge?
item.description: West Ham United survive a penalty scare against Leeds United after Max Kilman collides with Anton Stach after the German midfielder had already got his shot away.

item.title: Southampton shock Arsenal to reach FA Cup semi-finals
item.description: Arsenal are stung by a late goal from Southampton's Shea Charles, as the Premier League leaders fall to defeat in the FA Cup quarter-finals.

item.title: Charles scores late winner as Southampton beat Arsenal
item.description: Southampton secure a spot in the FA Cup semi-finals thanks to Shea Charles' winner in the 85th minute.

item.title: Rashford strikes as Barcelona beat Atletico Madrid
item.description: England forward Marcus Rashford scores an impressive goal in Barcelona's 2-1 win against Atletico Madrid.

item.title: Ranking football's greatest one-club men
item.description: From Francesco Totti to Matthew Le Tissier, one-club men share a rare and special bond with the fans - we've ranked the top 10 here.

item.title: 'Quiet and steady' - how Lincoln's promotion was 20 years in the making
item.description: John Pakey looks back over the past 20 years from Keith Alexander to the Cowleys to Lincoln City now calling themselves a Championship club.

item.title: Pollok United named best grassroots football club in Europe
item.description: Glasgow's Pollok United are named the best grassroots football club in Europe for their work providing local communities with opportunities in football and well-being.

item.title: Derby's Agyemang to miss World Cup with Achilles injury
item.description: Patrick Agyemang is ruled out of playing in a home World Cup for the USA after suffering a "serious" Achilles tendon injury in Derby win against Stoke.

item.title: Coventry edge ever closer - but who will join them in Premier League?
item.description: As Coventry edge ever closer to ending their 25-year wait for Premier League football, who is best placed to join them?

item.title: Why promoted Falkirk's superb season has created problem for league
item.description: Falkirk have upset the Scottish Premiership apple cart - and given the league's organisers a fixtures headache.

item.title: Quiz: Every team to reach a Women's FA Cup semi-final in 2020s
item.description: In total, 12 teams have reached the Women's FA Cup semi-finals since 2020. Can you name them all?

item.title: Is the Championship heading for financial 'catastrophe'?
item.description: BBC Sport takes a look at if the second tier could be facing a "doomsday scenario" as club accounts reveal the significant scale of their losses.

item.title: How former Liverpool prospect found love for football again
item.description: After 18 years at Liverpool, Tyler Morton tells BBC Sport how moving to Lyon in the summer has helped him fall back in love with football.

item.title: After more than 14 years at Atletico Madrid, what next for Simeone?
item.description: BBC Sport asks if Atletico Madrid could really part ways with manager Diego Simeone at the end of the season?

item.title: What De Zerbi needs to do to turn Tottenham around
item.description: Tony Pulis explores why Tottenham are in such a mess and what their new manager Roberto de Zerbi might do to change things as they seek to stay in the Premier League.

item.title: Kerr's uncertain future - should Chelsea keep striker?
item.description: The future of Chelsea striker Sam Kerr remains uncertain - but would it be a wise decision to let her leave on a free transfer this summer?

item.title: The World Cup line-up is complete - here's what you need to know
item.description: The final six nations have qualified for the 2026 World Cup - this is your essential lowdown on the expanded edition of the tournament.

item.title: All February and March's done deals, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: How to follow the FA Cup quarter-finals on the BBC
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explain how you can watch, listen and follow the FA Cup quarter-finals.

item.title: How does Scottish Premiership split work?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership title race has been a hot topic all season and it's about to become even more interesting with the league's split.

item.title: How does Tudor's record compare to other Tottenham bosses?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team compare Igor Tudor's managerial record to other Tottenham managers of recent years.

item.title: Have all home nations ever played in same World Cup?
item.description: With England and Scotland already qualified for the 2026 World Cup, Wales and Northern Ireland both face European qualifiers in March.

item.title: Who is one card away from suspension in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who is currently nearing a suspension in the Premier League.

item.title: All you need to know about 2027 Women's World Cup
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team take a look at everything you need to know for the 2027 Women's World Cup.

item.title: When can Arsenal win the Premier League?
item.description: Arsenal are nine points clear at the top of the Premier League, but when can they officially win the title?

item.title: 'Graham keen on Rangers, but Celtic still in race' - gossip
item.description: Dundee head coach Steven Pressley vows Luke Graham will not be sold on the cheap amid Old Firm interest, while Pan Camara is trying to persuade team-mate Amar Fatah to extend his stay with Dundee United.

item.title: McLeary replaces McAneny in Scotland squad
item.description: Rangers midfielder Jodi McLeary replaces Celtic counterpart Maria McAneny in the Scotland squad for next week's World Cup qualifying double header against Belgium.

item.title: Napoli warn they could punish Lukaku over training no-show
item.description: Striker Romelu Lukaku could face disciplinary action after Napoli released a statement to say he has ignored a request to return to the club.

item.title: Griezmann to join MLS side Orlando from Atletico
item.description: Former France forward Antoine Griezmann will join MLS side Orlando City from Atletico Madrid this summer.

item.title: PSG's league game between legs of Liverpool Champions League tie postponed
item.description: Paris St-Germain's Ligue One match against Lens, which was set to take place between the two legs of their Champions League quarter-final against Liverpool, has been postponed.

item.title: How Arbeloa is getting the best out of Real Madrid's stars
item.description: BBC Sport looks at how Alvaro Arbeloa has managed to get the best out of the Real Madrid squad since his appointment in January.

item.title: Real talk is nonsense but I may coach again - Klopp
item.description: Former Liverpool manager Jurgen Klopp says speculation he will return to management with Real Madrid this summer is "nonsense".

item.title: The 'Swiss Leicester' closing on their first title
item.description: A decade after Leicester unexpectedly won the Premier League, FC Thun of Switzerland are closing on one of football's greatest underdog triumphs.

item.title: Lang's surgery for thumb injury 'went well'
item.description: Galatasaray midfielder Noa Lang is set to have surgery in Liverpool after suffering a "serious cut" at Anfield.

item.title: 'An incredible league' - inside WSL 2 promotion race
item.description: With less than a month of the season remaining, BBC Sport assesses the key contenders battling for promotion from Women's Super League 2.

item.title: Reynolds receives first Republic of Ireland call-up
item.description: Shamrock Rovers defender Maria Reynolds receives her first call-up to the Republic of Ireland senior squad for the World Cup qualifying double-header against Poland.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?
